_notes: Published-literature fixtures for dcaEngine.literature.test.js (SC1, the
ReservoirEngineering-Module.md paragraph 3 oracle gate). Repo fixture
doctrine: values are typed from the source document (never recalled);
each case names its source, page and access date; known rounding
quirks are documented next to the numbers they affect.
Source for the four armed cases: Weaver, James R., P.E., 'Forecasting
Oil and Gas Using Decline Curves', CED Engineering continuing-education
course No. P03-004 (Continuing Education and Development, Inc.),
publicly served at
https://www.cedengineering.com/userfiles/Forecasting%20Oil%20and%20Gas%20Using%20Decline%20Curves.pdf
fetched and read page-by-page on 2026-07-18. The document works every
example in the Arps nominal-decline forms the engine implements
(rate q = qi/(1+b*di*t)^(1/b); cumulative between rates
Np = qi^b/(di*(1-b))*(qi^(1-b) - q^(1-b)); harmonic and exponential
limits), with EFFECTIVE annual declines de converted to nominal via
di = -ln(1 - de). Nominal declines scale linearly with time units, so
the engine's per-day Di is di_per_year / 365 and engine cumulative
volumes equal the document's printed 365-multiplied Np directly.
Rounding doctrine per case: printed RATES are rounded (e.g. 99 bbl/d
for 98.73), while printed CUMULATIVES come from the author's unrounded
spreadsheet. Tests therefore recompute the unrounded end rate from the
rate equation and compare cumulatives against the printed Np with a
tolerance stated in the case, and compare printed rates with an
absolute rounding allowance.
PENDING (the two references paragraph 3 names; suite lists them as
jest todo entries and SC2 notes the gate in SCALStudio-STATUS.md):
SPEE Recommended Evaluation Practice #6 and Poston & Poe, 'Analysis of
Production Decline Curves' (SPE, 2008) are paid documents. When the
owner supplies the PDFs, their example problems are typed here as
additional cases with the same provenance discipline.
pending_references: SPEE Recommended Evaluation Practice #6 (decline-curve estimation) - owner to supply PDF
Poston & Poe, Analysis of Production Decline Curves, SPE 2008 - owner to supply PDF

given.note: nominal di = -ln(1 - 0.15) = 0.16252 per year; the document's Np formula 365*(qi-q)/(-ln(1-d)) is the Arps exponential cumulative with per-day Di

cases.reference: Weaver P03-004, p. 14 (shale gas hyperbolic example)

given.note: document prints di = -ln(1-0.65) = 1.0498 per year; b > 1 exercises the (1-b) < 0 branch of the cumulative formula (the branch the pre-SC1 sign bug inverted)

published.note: the document prints both cumulative forms and attributes their 82 Mscf spread to round-off of the 876 rate; tests compare against the time form, whose inputs are unrounded

cases.reference: Weaver P03-004, pp. 21-22 (harmonic rate-cumulative example, daily-rate table)

given.note: document prints yearly di = -ln(1-0.35) = 0.4308; the 11-row table below is typed verbatim from the printed daily-rate table (Qo rounded to whole bbl/d, Np from the author's unrounded spreadsheet)

cases.reference: Weaver P03-004, pp. 18-19 (hyperbolic-to-exponential example, hyperbolic leg)

given.note: monthly-rate form; the document derives qi = 150 bbl/d * 365/12 = 4563 bbl/m and monthly di = -ln(1-0.0530) = 0.0545. Nominal declines convert linearly, so the engine runs qi = 4563 volume/month-units with Di per month directly (t in months)
